title: Wrongly handled definer for triggers when PS used
poc: CREATE TABLE c (id int primary key, a varchar(100));
INSERT INTO c SET id =1, a=now();
CREATE TABLE tbl01 (id int primary key, a varchar(100));
CREATE TABLE tbl02 (id int primary key, a varchar(100));
GRANT SELECT, UPDATE, INSERT on test.tbl01 TO 'user02'@'%';
INSERT INTO tbl01 SET id = 1, a=now();
UPDATE ON tbl01 FOR EACH ROW UPDATE tbl02 SET a = (SELECT a FROM test01.c WHERE id =1) WHERE id=NEW.id;
prepare s from 'update tbl01 set a = now()';
key_bug_features: Definer triggers causing permission errors with prepared statements
bug_features_seed: GRANT SELECT, INSERT ON test01.c TO 'user03'@'%';
CREATE TABLE tbl03 (id int primary key, b int NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, c varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL);
INSERT INTO tbl03 (id, b) VALUES (1, NULL);
CREATE DEFINER='user01'@'%' TRIGGER trg02 BEFORE INSERT ON tbl03 FOR EACH ROW SET NEW.c = (SELECT a FROM test01.c WHERE id = 1);
